name: crew
description: Autonomous, GitHub-issue-driven dev workflow for Claude Code. A looping orchestrator (/crew:run) pulls agent-ready issues and drives each through bundled implementation, qa, reviewer, mr-review, and findings subagents — one MR per ticket, GitHub as the source of truth. Ships /crew:ticket (issue writer + condense mode that batches review-followup tickets), /crew:pulls (autonomous merge loop that runs alongside /crew:run and absorbs the jobs of /crew:merge + /crew:approve — it drains the ready-for-review MR queue by merging them itself with no human green-light, inverting the control surface so merge is the default and the only brake is a human comment: a block/question parks the MR — the comment-driven control surface covers both PR comments and review threads — and the human releases it by resolving the thread or removing the hold label; driven by the pull-triage agent (the one cross-MR brain that surveys all open MRs and writes a per-run triage tracking issue, opened at run start and closed at run end) and the merge-judge agent (per-MR independent decision + question responder), with conflict/CI fixes dispatched to crew:implementation and main healed in-loop), /crew:merge (team-lead merge queue: merges approved-labeled MRs, resolves conflicts on all open MRs regardless of label, and optionally auto-merges the provably-low-risk slice), /crew:approve (auto-approves the low-risk MRs so /crew:merge can land them), /crew:improve (codebase audit → backlog tickets), and /crew:adjust (project onboarding). Optionally runs under a dedicated GitHub App bot identity (opt-in crew-identity) so its comments/commits show as a bot. The run loop selects tickets by priority then age.
